Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section explicitly lists PGDH1 as At4g34200.

Source_Species.reason: AtPGDH1 is an Arabidopsis gene and Arabidopsis materials were used.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material section states Arabidopsis ecotype Columbia-0.

Applied_Species.reason: AtPGDH1 was overexpressed in Arabidopsis and also heterologously overexpressed in maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper uses Arabidopsis thaliana and maize; maize corresponds to Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Columbia-0 is supported for Arabidopsis, but no maize variety/genotype is specified in the provided text.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number 1.1.1.95 appears in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PGDH family is supported, but the specific domain label 'dehydrogenase' as a family/domain annotation is not explicitly given.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: AtPGDH1 is described as part of the Phosphorylated Pathway of Serine Biosynthesis (PPSB).

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: PGDH is one of the three core PPSB enzymes, so core serine biosynthesis pathway is directly inferable.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Summary states PPSB sequential reactions are catalysed by PGDH, PSAT and PSP, placing PGDH first/upstream.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Results and Discussion explicitly argue PGDH1 is the rate-limiting/limiting reaction in Arabidopsis PPSB based on AtPGDH1 versus triple Oex comparisons.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Discussion explicitly states 'The PGDH reaction uses 3-PGA as a substrate'.

Primary_Product.reason: 3-phosphohydroxypyruvate is not named in the paper text provided.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The first-step role in PPSB from 3-phosphoglycerate is supported, but the full explicit reaction wording including oxidation to 3-phosphohydroxypyruvate is not stated in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results support that AtPGDH1 overexpression raises Ser and downstream amino acids/protein, so precursor-supply/initiating-pathway connection is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly show links to serine, protein, essential amino acids, and in Arabidopsis starch.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Those endpoints are amino acids, protein, and starch/carbohydrate as shown in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary/Conclusion explicitly frame the outcome as improved nutritional value via increased nitrogen/protein content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Arabidopsis measurements were in shoots; maize measurements were in leaves, stems, and kernels.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both ambient CO2 and elevated CO2 (2000 ppm) are explicitly used.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states Arabidopsis AtPGDH1/2 are more expressed in roots than shoots and cites prior work for shoot up-regulation under elevated CO2 and darkness.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and maize is directly described; phenotypes were assessed in Arabidopsis shoots and maize leaves, stems, and kernels.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All components are supported by Results: Ser and protein increase in Arabidopsis; starch increase in Arabidopsis; attenuation of elevated-CO2 amino-acid decline; maize protein/N:C increase without starch increase.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all described and used in Results: transgenic overexpression, comparison with triple Oex, qRT-PCR, western blot, GC-MS, protein/starch assays, elemental analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed finding is directly supported by the Results/figures for AtPGDH1 in Arabidopsis and maize.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state At OexPGDH1 had greater starch effect than At TriOex; maize high-expression line L2 showed 17% growth decrease.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: General engineering value for improving nitrogen/protein under elevated CO2 is supported, but specific suggestions about stacking with other PPSB genes or sink/transport traits are speculative beyond direct Results.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Possible maize growth penalty in highest expressing line is in Results; altered carbon partitioning via starch increase in Arabidopsis is directly observed, though framed interpretively.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Oryza sativa Ubiquitin 3 promoter for maize is explicitly stated; Arabidopsis promoter information is not given here, so 'NA in Arabidopsis lines in provided text' is meta rather than a paper-supported biological field.

Omics_Data.reason: Metabolite profiling by GC-MS is explicitly described; no public accession is reported in the provided text.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PSAT1 and PSP1 are pathway partners co-overexpressed with AtPGDH1, but direct physical protein-protein interaction is not shown.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental procedures list AtPSP1 as At1g18640.

Applied_Species.reason: AtPSP1 itself was applied in Arabidopsis; maize experiments targeted the maize ortholog PSP1, not AtPSP1 directly.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is supported, but application of AtPSP1 to Zea mays is not shown.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Columbia-0 is supported for Arabidopsis; no maize variety is specified and AtPSP1 was not applied in maize.

EC_Number.reason: EC 3.1.3.3 is not stated in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not mention the haloacid dehalogenase-like hydrolase superfamily annotation for AtPSP1.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: AtPSP1 is explicitly one of the three PPSB genes.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As one of the three PPSB enzymes, core serine biosynthesis pathway is directly inferable.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Summary lists PSP after PGDH and PSAT, making it the downstream terminal step.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Results/Discussion indicate PSP1 did not have a considerable additional effect under ambient CO2, supporting that it is not limiting there.

Primary_Substrate.reason: 3-phosphoserine is not explicitly named in the paper text.

Primary_Product.reason: The pathway is explicitly for serine biosynthesis and PSP is the final enzyme, so L-serine as product is directly inferable.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Its terminal role in generating serine is inferable, but the explicit dephosphorylation wording is not present in the paper text.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PSP1 is the terminal PPSB enzyme producing serine, so direct connection to serine is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Arabidopsis triple lines connect PSP1 to serine/protein/essential amino acid outcomes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are amino acids and protein.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Improved amino acid/protein content is supported for Arabidopsis triple lines, but the specific maize pollen viability statement pertains to ZmPSP1 rather than AtPSP1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Arabidopsis shoots were measured; maize PSP1 context is male gametophyte/pollen, though that is ortholog-based rather than AtPSP1 itself.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Ambient/elevated CO2 and serine supplementation in pollen assays are all in the paper, though serine supplementation applies to maize PSP1 phenotype.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: AtPSP1 was manipulated in Arabidopsis triple overexpression lines; maize endogenous PSP1 requirement in male gametophyte is also described, though for ZmPSP1.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Arabidopsis triple-overexpression effect is supported, but attributing the maize loss-of-function pollen phenotype to AtPSP1 conflates AtPSP1 with ZmPSP1 ortholog evidence.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Arabidopsis triple overexpression is valid for AtPSP1, but maize Cas9 mutagenesis/segregation/serine rescue validate ZmPSP1, not AtPSP1 directly.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Arabidopsis items are supported, but the listed maize mutation findings belong to ZmPSP1 rather than AtPSP1 itself.

Other_Important_Info.reason: GRMZM2g165383 and essential SGG motif are maize PSP1-specific, not AtPSP1-specific.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Caution about disrupting PSP1 is supported for maize PSP1, but combining that with AtPSP1-based nutrient engineering is only partly supported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Male sterility/gametophyte lethality is supported for maize PSP1 disruption, not AtPSP1 directly.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AtPGDH1 and AtPSAT1 are pathway partners, but no direct interactions are shown.
